externalDeps.note: none — manifest declares zero dependencies and zero devDependencies (lockfile confirms: packages {}); a zero-dependency layer is a defining fact, not an omission. Tests run on the built-in node:test runner (npm test = 'node --test'), so even testing adds no external dependency.

stackRefinements.refinement: Zero-dependency Node.js >= 20 ESM CLI (type: module, .mjs sources, bin 'wordy' -> src/cli_main.mjs); tests via built-in node:test runner. Stack relies solely on the Node standard library — no framework, ORM, or build tool.

ordering: Single layer (cli at '.') with internalEdges: [] — no provider/consumer relationships exist, so there is no cross-layer ordering constraint. The cli layer is an island (no other layer consumes it), which is an ordering observation, not a gap: any task dispatches directly to cli with no required predecessor.
